# fix(portal): discussions news timeline canvas blank / not mounting on navigation

## Context

Operator-reported (2026-06-01): in the Portal news **discussions** view, the offscreen-canvas "Neural Timeline" is broken — the timeline canvas does not render when a discussion is opened. Tickets + pulls timelines render correctly.

## Live Diagnosis (Neural Link, operator instance)

Observed on the running Portal (session `1b783d4e`), navigating to `#/news/discussions/12100`:
- The discussions content Component (`Portal.view.news.discussions.Component`, `neo-markdown-1`) **and** its `Portal.view.content.TimelineCanvas` (`neo-canvas-2`) were both `mounted: false` (`hidden: false`).
- The StateProvider had resolved correctly: `currentPageRecord = 12100`, `countSections = 14`, 14 timeline records present.
- **Zero console errors.**
- For comparison, the pulls `TimelineCanvas` (`neo-canvas-3`) was `mounted: true` when the pulls view was active.

So it is **not** malformed data and **not** the `countSections < 1` container-collapse path.

## Open Question (determines the fix layer)

The diagnosis is ambiguous between two layers — the deciding signal is the **visible symptom**:
- **Article text present, only the canvas/timeline blank** → canvas-mount/remount bug (prime suspect: `OffscreenCanvas` can only be `transferControlToOffscreen()` once, so a view unmounted on navigation may fail to re-establish its offscreen context on re-visit).
- **Whole content panel blank** → view-activation bug (the discussions card/route not mounting the content Component).

(A `set_route`-driven Neural Link repro left both unmounted, but couldn't be cleanly distinguished from a `set_route`-vs-real-click artifact — the `TabContainer` activeIndex query timed out. A click-path symptom or a careful live re-investigation resolves it.)

## Suspect
`#12308` ("structure discussion replies") recently reshaped discussions timeline population — a candidate regression window.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AC1 — Opening a discussion renders its timeline canvas (the "Neural Timeline") the same as tickets/pulls.
- [ ] AC2 — Root cause identified (canvas remount vs view activation) + fixed at that layer.
- [ ] AC3 — If it's an OffscreenCanvas remount issue, a regression guard for unmount→remount of the shared TimelineCanvas.
- [ ] AC4 — L3: navigate away from a discussion and back; the canvas re-renders (no stale/blank canvas).

## Out of Scope
- Tickets/pulls timeline canvas (rendering correctly).

## Related
- Epic #12204 (Portal news views). Surfaced by the operator alongside the news-section polish.

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-06-01T19:03:52Z

## Diagnosis correction — canvas MOUNTS; this is a RENDER bug, not mount/view (Neural Link, fresh session)

Re-investigated on a fresh Portal session with a discussion open via **real navigation** (not `set_route`):
- discussions content Component (`neo-markdown-1`): `mounted: true`
- its `TimelineCanvas` (`neo-canvas-2`): `mounted: true`, `offscreenRegistered: true`, 14 timeline records, **no console errors**

So my earlier "both `mounted: false`" was the `set_route`-vs-real-click artifact (mid-transition), **not** the bug. **Ruled out:** view-activation, canvas mount, offscreen registration, data, console errors.

→ **B is a render-layer bug**: the offscreen canvas is mounted + registered + fed records, but the drawn timeline is visually wrong. Neural Link is introspection-only (no offscreen screenshot), so the exact failure mode needs the **visible symptom**:
- **Blank/empty canvas** → sizing (0-dim offscreen) or draw-loop no-op
- **Nodes present but misaligned** → node-position mapping (the canvas aligns nodes to `.neo-timeline-item[data-record-id]` DOM rects; discussions item DOM may differ)
- **No spine / wrong colors** → palette/geometry (discussions records carry `{iconCls|image, name, tag}` — no `color`, unlike ticket *events*)
- **Stale (previous discussion's timeline)** → re-render-on-record-change

(AC2 refined: root cause is in the canvas **render** layer, not mount.)


### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-06-01T20:53:12Z

## Root cause — empirically confirmed via Neural Link (2026-06-01)

The spine renders bogus far-left segments (operator: "~20° angle to the left from the last item", "a 2nd spine comes in from the left and meets the first at the last item") because the timeline canvas coordinator builds nodes from **zero-size DOM rects**.

**Evidence** (live discussion 11891, `Portal.view.content.TimelineCanvas#neo-canvas-2`): of 5 timeline items, the 3 below-the-fold ones return zero rects while the canvas overlay sits at `x:408`:

| element | rect |
| :--- | :--- |
| `timeline-11891-0-target` | x=466, y=-3650, 40×40 ✓ |
| `timeline-11891-1-target` | x=466, y=-1397, 40×40 ✓ |
| `timeline-11891-2/3/4-target` (+ the item containers `timeline-11891-2/3/4`) | **{x:0,y:0,w:0,h:0}** ✗ |
| canvas `neo-canvas-2` | x=**408**, y=-3931, 689×8706 |

**Mechanism:**
1. Discussion comment bodies render through the **Markdown component**, which sets `content-visibility: auto` + a `content-visibility 300ms allow-discrete` / `block-size 300ms` transition — `src/component/Markdown.scss:37,40`. Off-screen comment items are layout-skipped → zero rects. (This transition is the "animation only on navigation" the operator sees; first page-load has fewer/no transitioning items.)
2. `Portal.view.content.TimelineCanvas#onTimelineDataLoad` guards node creation with only `if (rect)`. A zero rect `{x:0,y:0,w:0,h:0}` is truthy → it pushes a node at `nodeX = rect.x(0) - canvasRect.x(408) = -408` (far left), `nodeY = 0 - canvasRect.y`. These far-left bogus nodes are the broken spine; the renderer (`Portal.canvas.TimelineCanvas`) just draws `moveTo/lineTo` through them.
3. `waitForDomRect` only retries on zero-*size* (`rect.height < 1 || rect.width < 1`); off-screen content-visibility items stay zero → it returns the zero rect after exhausting 20 attempts.
4. `ensureFinalAlignment` (the post-settle re-capture) is wired only to `onResize`, NOT to navigation/scroll → the bogus nodes are never corrected.

Discussions-specific because long markdown bodies + nested replies make the content very tall (8706px here) → most items off-screen → content-visibility-skipped. Tickets/pulls have short content, so few/no off-screen skips.

## Fix plan
1. **Reject zero-size rects** in `onTimelineDataLoad`'s node-builder (`if (rect && rect.width > 0 && rect.height > 0)`) — no far-left bogus nodes; the spine draws correctly through the measurable items.
2. **Re-align as items become measurable** — fire the debounced re-align (`ensureFinalAlignment`) on navigation/data-load (currently only `onResize`), and on scroll, so off-screen items get correct nodes once their `content-visibility` resolves and the spine extends properly.
3. Do NOT touch the Markdown `content-visibility` (intentional perf optimization) — make the canvas robust to it.

Verification: unit coverage for zero-rect rejection in the node-builder + L3 visual confirmation on the running instance (operator) post-fix.

Separate discussions issue (markdown renders unescaped raw HTML as literal strings) is delegated to @neo-gpt — tracked separately, not part of this canvas fix.

— @neo-opus-ada (claude-opus-4.8-1m) · session da9a6007

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-06-01T21:28:57Z

**[L3-deferred — operator handoff needed]**

PR #12327 implements the canvas-side fix: the timeline-canvas coordinator now rejects zero-size DOM rects (the far-left / 2nd-spine root cause — below-fold items return `{0,0,0,0}` rects from collapsed `<details>` / lazy avatars / mid-transition, and the old `if (rect)` guard turned them into nodes at `x = -canvasRect.x`) and re-aligns on navigation. Root cause + tests are in the PR.

The fix is **unit-verified (L2)** but carries a residual **L3 visual** AC the agent sandbox cannot reach: confirming the discussions timeline spine renders clean on the live Portal surface (no ~20° left angle, no second spine from the left, no breakage during the navigation animation).

PR #12327 deliberately uses `Related: #12322` (not `Resolves`) so the merge does **not** auto-close this issue. **This issue stays open until the operator confirms the L3 visual**, then closes it — which closes epic #12204.

Post-merge verification expectation: operator L3 visual on the running Portal instance. If a long discussion still shows the spine not extending to below-fold items while scrolling, that's a scroll-triggered re-capture follow-up (separate sub).

— @neo-opus-ada
